title: NYC Rent vs Buy — Review analysis and decide next steps
description: Review the rent vs buy analysis. Key findings: (1) Rent first for 12 months while setting up a 10b5-1 plan to liquidate MNTN stock. (2) Combined NYC tax hit on stock sales is ~38.5% — need to sell ~$500K to net $300K for a down payment. (3) Co-ops are out due to primary residence/subletting restrictions. (4) Look at condos in $1.0-1.3M range after building liquid position. (5) Consider furnished rental services (Blueground, Sonder) for maximum flexibility while traveling. Next steps: talk to a financial advisor about 10b5-1 plan, talk to a real estate attorney, start exploring neighborhoods.

title: Anniversary plan for Friday
description: I have a dinner booked at 5pm st soothr. Need plans after. Cabernet, comedy, weird shows, something to do.

comments.text:
## Anniversary After-Dinner Plans — Options Near Soothr (204 E 13th St, East Village)

Dinner is at 5pm at Soothr, so you'll likely be done around 6:30–7pm. Here are curated after-dinner options:

### Wine Bars (Cabernet Focus)
1. **Ruffian** — Tiny East Village wine bar, 250+ natural wines, Michelin Bib Gourmand. Walk from Soothr. Great intimate anniversary vibe.
2. **Stars** — New spot from the Claud/Penny team. 1,000-bottle list, by-the-glass from $11, homage to classic East Village wine bars. Very close to Soothr.
3. **Ten Bells** (LES) — Cozy, dark, laid-back natural wine bar. Short walk east.

### Comedy Shows
1. **New York Comedy Club** (E 4th St) — Walking distance from Soothr. Multiple shows nightly, check newyorkcomedyclub.com for April 10 lineup.
2. **Greenwich Village Comedy Club** — Also nearby in the Village, intimate setting.
3. **Broadway Comedy Club** — Near Times Square, top headliner lineups from Netflix/Comedy Central acts.

### Weird/Unique Experiences
1. **Batsu** — Japanese game show-style challenges with authentic food & drinks, inspired by Tokyo alleyways. Fun, interactive, memorable.
2. **The Office of Mr. Motto** — Private museum + 21-course omakase with a cryptic password to enter. Very unique anniversary-worthy experience.
3. **SPYSCAPE** (Midtown) — Interactive spy museum with surveillance, lie detection, hacking challenges. Fun couples activity.
4. **Swingers NoMad** — Crazy mini-golf with craft cocktails, 21+. Lighthearted and fun.

### Suggested Flow
**Option A (Romantic):** Soothr → Stars or Ruffian for wine → walk through East Village
**Option B (Fun):** Soothr → Batsu (game show) → nightcap at Ten Bells
**Option C (Adventure):** Soothr → SPYSCAPE or Swingers → cocktails at a nearby bar

activity_log.details: Researched after-dinner anniversary plans near Soothr in East Village. Posted curated options (wine bars, comedy, unique experiences) and 3 suggested evening flows as comments. Assigned back to Christopher.

title: Test race gear in New York
Review 50-Mile Race Nutrition & Hydration Plan
description:
## 50-MILE RACE NUTRITION PLAN — HOT & HUMID CONDITIONS (90°F)

### HOURLY TARGETS
- Carbs: 60-80g per hour (higher end due to heat increasing glycogen burn)
- Calories: 250-320 cal per hour
- Sodium: 800-1200mg per hour (critical in heat/humidity — sweat losses can exceed 1500mg/hr)
- Water: 800-1000ml per hour (20-30oz). Drink to thirst but don't fall behind. In 90°F humidity you'll lose 1-2 liters/hour through sweat.

### HOUR-BY-HOUR FOOD PLAN

**Miles 1-15 (Hours 0-2.5) — Start Early, Stay Ahead**
- Every 30 min: 1 gel (25g carbs, ~100 cal) + 2-3 sips sports drink
- Every hour: 1 SaltStick cap (215mg sodium) or 2 if sweating heavy
- Water: alternate sips between plain water and sports drink mix
- DON'T skip fueling because you feel good — heat catches up fast

**Miles 15-30 (Hours 2.5-5) — Mix It Up**
- Alternate gels with real food every other feeding:
  - PB&J squares (cut into 2-inch pieces, wrapped in foil): ~150 cal, 20g carbs each
  - Boiled baby potatoes dipped in salt: ~80 cal, easy on stomach
  - Rice balls with salt: ~120 cal, 25g carbs
- Continue 1 electrolyte cap every 30-45 min
- Add a LMNT or similar high-sodium drink packet to one flask
- If stomach feels off: switch to liquid calories only (Tailwind, Maurten mix)

**Miles 30-40 (Hours 5-7) — The Grind**
- Whatever sounds good — sweet or savory, just keep eating
- Deploy caffeine: 100mg caffeinated gel at mile 30, another at mile 38
- Cola at aid stations: easy caffeine + sugar + settles stomach
- Broth: warm sodium bomb, surprisingly easy to stomach
- Watermelon: hydration + sugar + potassium (grab at every aid station offering it)
- Continue electrolyte caps religiously

**Miles 40-50 (Hours 7-9+) — Bring It Home**
- Liquid calories are fine if solids won't stay down
- Second caffeine push if needed (stay under 400mg total race)
- Cola, broth, ginger ale are your friends
- Keep sipping water — dehydration compounds exponentially in the final miles

### HYDRATION STRATEGY FOR EXTREME HEAT
- Pre-load: drink 500ml with electrolytes 2 hours before start
- Carry: 2 x 500ml soft flasks (one water, one sports drink)
- Refill at EVERY aid station even if flasks aren't empty
- Ice: stuff ice in hat, bandana around neck, and down shirt at aid stations — cooling is as important as hydration
- Pour water on head and wrists at every opportunity
- Monitor urine: if you haven't peed in 3+ hours or it's very dark, increase intake immediately

### SODIUM IS KING IN THE HEAT
- Target 800-1200mg/hour (vs normal 500-700mg in cool weather)
- Sources: SaltStick caps (215mg each = take 4-5/hour), LMNT packets (1000mg), salty food at aid stations, sports drink mix
- Signs you're low: bloating, nausea, swollen fingers, confusion, muscle cramps
- Carry extra salt caps in a ziplock in your vest — do not run out

### WHAT TO BUY (SHOPPING LIST)
- 15-20 gels (mix flavors: some caffeinated for miles 30+)
- 2 boxes energy chews as backup
- SaltStick caps (full bottle of 100 — you'll use 40-50)
- LMNT or Liquid IV packets (6-8)
- Sports drink powder (Tailwind, Skratch, or Maurten)
- PB&J supplies (white bread, smooth PB, jam)
- Baby potatoes + salt
- Sushi rice + salt for rice balls
- Ginger chews (nausea backup)
- Body glide (chafing is worse in humidity)
- Cooling bandana or ice bandana
- Spare hat (white/light colored)

### HEAT-SPECIFIC WARNINGS
- Start 15-20% slower than cool-weather goal pace — heat costs you, period
- Walk all uphills from mile 1 — save energy for thermoregulation
- If you stop sweating, feel confused, or get chills: STOP, cool down, seek medical. These are heat illness red flags.
- Avoid NSAIDs (ibuprofen/Advil) — they impair kidney function and worsen heat illness risk
- Pre-cool: cold towel on neck before start, cold water in flasks

activity_log.details: Task created with detailed 50-mile race nutrition plan for hot/humid conditions
